text.title: decision-framework Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decision-framework as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using RAG when the corpus is under 50 documents that fit in a single long-context prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: RAG adds retrieval latency, chunking artifacts, and index maintenance overhead. For small static corpora, stuffing everything into a single prompt is simpler and more accurate.

failure-mode.description: Committing to fine-tuning without calculating ROI: training cost + 6x inference cost + dataset maintenance vs quality gain vs volume.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fine-tuning decisions are hard to reverse short-term. The dataset must be maintained and retraining must happen as the base model updates.
